title: Build core MLM member dashboard and admin panel (Phase 1)

requirements.text: Implement the backend data model and logic for the 3×3 matrix MLM system: store members with fields (id, name, mobile, email, sponsorId, uplineId, position in matrix, joinDate, status: active/debarred/suspended/deleted, paymentStatus: pending/paid), enforce a maximum of 3 direct downline slots per member, and auto-assign new members to the next available slot under their sponsor using breadth-first placement.
acceptanceCriteria: Each member node has at most 3 direct children in the matrix
New members are placed in the correct next-available slot under their sponsor
Member status can be active, debarred, suspended, or deleted
Payment status tracks pending vs paid joining fee

requirements.text: Implement backend commission calculation: joining fee is ₹2,750 per member. Level 1 (3 members): full fee refund of ₹2,750. Levels 2–9: calculate commissions as (number of members at that depth) × ₹2,750 × percentage (Level 2: 9%, Level 3: 8%, Level 4: 7%, Level 5: 6%, Level 6: 5%, Level 7: 4%, Level 8: 3%, Level 9: 1%). Expose a query to return per-level earnings and total balance for any given member.
acceptanceCriteria: Level 1 earnings equal ₹2,750 (fee refund) when 3 direct downline members are present
Levels 2–9 compute correctly using the defined percentages
Per-level breakdown and total balance are returned for any member ID

requirements.text: Implement backend member management operations: (a) admin can add a new member directly, (b) members can self-register with a sponsor ID, (c) admin can debar or suspend a member, (d) admin can delete a member. When a member is removed or debarred, reassign their direct downline children to the removed member's own upline, filling the vacated slot first.

quotes: Admin Powers: Omit, delete, or debar/suspend any member

acceptanceCriteria: Admin can create a member record directly
Self-registration assigns sponsor and places member in matrix
Debarring or suspending updates member status without removing their record
Deleting a member triggers downline reassignment to the deleted member's upline

requirements.text: Build an individual member dashboard page showing: (a) a summary card with total balance and fee refund status, (b) a per-level commission breakdown table (levels 1–9 with member count, percentage, and computed earning), (c) a visual 3×3 matrix tree showing their downline up to 9 levels with member name/status on each node and empty slot indicators, (d) quick stats (total downline count, levels filled).

quotes: Member Dashboard — earnings, downline tree, per-level amounts, total balance

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays total earnings and fee refund status
Commission table shows all 9 levels with correct values
Matrix tree renders up to 9 levels with member cards and empty slots
Quick stats reflect current downline count

requirements.text: Build an admin panel page with: (a) a searchable, paginated members table showing name, mobile, email, sponsor, status, payment status, and join date, (b) action buttons per member row to mark fee as paid, debar, suspend, or delete a member, (c) platform statistics cards (total members, total fees collected, total refunds issued, total commissions paid), (d) an 'Add Member' form for admin-initiated registration.

quotes: Admin Panel — add/manage members, view all calculations

acceptanceCriteria: Members table is searchable by name or ID and paginated
Admin can mark payment as paid from the table row
Admin can debar, suspend, or delete any member from the table row
Platform statistics cards update to reflect current data
Add Member form submits and places the new member in the matrix

requirements.text: Build a self-registration page where a new member can enter their name, mobile number, email, and sponsor ID, validate the sponsor ID exists in the system, and submit to create their account pending payment approval.
acceptanceCriteria: Registration form validates all required fields
Sponsor ID is validated against existing members before submission
Successful registration shows a confirmation message and pending payment status
Invalid sponsor ID shows a clear error message

requirements.text: Ensure the member login flow (via Internet Identity) correctly identifies whether the authenticated user is a registered member or admin, redirects members to their dashboard, redirects admins to the admin panel, and redirects unregistered users to the registration page.
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated members land on their personal dashboard
Authenticated admins land on the admin panel
Unregistered authenticated users are prompted to register

constraints: Do not implement Stripe payment integration or payment proof upload in this phase — payment features will be added in a follow-up build
Keep all Motoko logic in the single backend/main.mo actor file
nonGoals: Stripe online payment integration (deferred to next phase)
Payment proof upload and admin approval workflow (deferred to next phase)
